title: Micranthemum bryoides
taxonomy: Micranthemum bryoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the moss carpet plant or moss stonecrop. It is native to South America and has been widely cultivated as an aquatic plant for its attractive foliage and delicate white flowers. The plant is often used in aquariums and water gardens due to its ability to thrive in a variety of water conditions. Micranthemum bryoides is a relatively small plant, typically growing to a height of 5-15 cm, and can be propagated through cuttings or division. Its classification also places it within the genus Micranthemum, which includes several other species of similar plants.

intro: Micranthemum bryoides, commonly known as rockweed or bog button, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. Bioactive constituents of Micranthemum bryoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
